mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60d returns in > 55% of occurrences, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an economic expansion regime reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, creating short-term positive momentum for S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during expansion regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical separation from zero (p > 0.05) in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 220.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of threshold-crossing instances, or Rank IC drops below -0.01 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high real GDP growth and elevated consumer prices create a stagflationary environment that forces monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term yields positive predictive power (Rank IC > 0.05) over 20-day horizons, or joint signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with > 50% accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening regime signals supply-side contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, triggering short-term risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn positive in > 60% of triggered instances during tightening regimes, or regime-conditional signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward regression.
